title: Fraud as defined in the Fraud Act 2006
summary: 9084 Criminal — fraud by false representation, failing to disclose, and abuse of position.
learningObjectives: Analyse failing to disclose (s3) and abuse of position (s4).
Apply dishonesty, gain/loss, and causation elements.

simpleExplanation.title: Modern fraud offences
simpleExplanation.summary: The Fraud Act 2006 creates three routes to fraud — lying, staying silent when there is a legal duty, or abusing a trusted position — all require dishonest intent to gain or cause loss.
steps: s2 — false representation, express or implied.
s4 — abuse of position of trust.

sections.content: **Fraud Act 2006** (2.2.8) completes property offences. Compare **s2 vs s3 vs s4** routes.
